stories.headline: Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
stories.blurb: In Rewriting Bun in Rust Jarred Sumner made the following claim: Claude Code v2.1.181 (released June 17th) and later use the Rust port of Bun. Startup got 10% faster on Linux but otherwise, barely anyone noticed. Boring is good. I decided to have a poke at my own Claude Code installation to see if I could find evidence that it was using Bun written in Rust. I found these two commands convincing: s

stories.headline: SQLite Query Explainer
stories.blurb: Tool: SQLite Query Explainer Julia Evan's, in Learning a few things about running SQLite : Maybe one day I’ll learn to read a query plan. Big same.... which inspired me to have Fable build this interactive explain tool, which runs SQLite in Python in Pyodide in Web Assembly in the browser and adds a layer of explanation to the results of both EXPLAIN and EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN. Approach with caution,

stories.headline: Claude make Fable 5 permanent
stories.blurb: Claude make Fable 5 permanent An update from the @claudeai account on Twitter: Beginning July 20, Claude Fable 5 will be included in all Max and Team Premium plans, at 50% of limits. Pro and Team Standard users will continue to have access to Fable via usage credits, and will receive a one-time $100 credit. As I was saying last week , the competition from GPT-5.6 Sol (and maybe to a lesser extent

stories.headline: nascheme/quixote
stories.blurb: nascheme/quixote A certain vintage if Python web nerd might be delighted to learn that the most recent commit to the Quixote web framework was six hours ago . The oldest commit in that repo is from 21 years ago, and that was the initial import of Quixote 2.4 from Subversion into Git. Tags: computer-history , python , web-frameworks

stories.headline: Quoting Kimi K3
stories.blurb: Is there something I can actually help you with today? — Kimi K3 , after refusing to leak its system prompt Tags: kimi , ai-personality , generative-ai , ai , llms

stories.headline: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
stories.blurb: Four people are killed and at least 36 injured as Ukraine's two largest cities are targeted, as Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

stories.headline: Violence repeatedly erupts in dementia care despite warnings, inspections show
stories.blurb: Nursing homes and assisted living facilities can be fertile ground for violence between residents with dementia, including fatal assaults. Court records and inspecitions how safeguards fall short.

stories.headline: 'Corny On The Bob' A Rising TikTok Meme Thanks To This TikToker's Excellent Meal Showcase Videos
stories.blurb: Corny On the Bob refers to a series of memes and video edits of TikToker Patty Miller (@pattyzapfemiller) calling corn and corn on the cob "corny on the bob" while showing off what she's eating for her meals. Videos of Patty calling corn on the cob "corny on the bob," and calling corn off the cob "corny off the bob," date back as early as May 2025. Clips of her using the term were popularized as t

stories.headline: For Over A Decade, This 'Billy Madison' Scene Has Been A Popular Reaction Meme For Adults Who Love Kids' Movies
stories.blurb: Adam Sandler Sitting With Kids or Billy Madison Sitting In First Grade Classroom is a reaction image and exploitable image macro meme format based on a scene from the 1995 Adam Sandler film Billy Madison where Billy Madison sits in his first-grade classroom for story time, surrounded by his classmates, children. The image was first used as a reaction meme as early as 2014 and became increasingly p

stories.headline: The First 'If X Has a Million Fans, I'm One of Them' Meme Was Shared On Facebook 13 Years Ago Today
stories.blurb: If X Has a Million Fans, I'm One of Them is a copypasta and phrasal template in which a diehard fan of a famous person declares that they will remain their fan to their very last breath even if the entire world turns against that person. Spread as a copypasta about football player Cristiano Ronaldo on Facebook since 2013, the copypasta later gained virality in Twitch chat spam, and later with a ve

stories.headline: Jennifer Finch, L7 Bassist, Dead At 59
stories.blurb: Jennifer Finch of L7 has died. The musician joined the Los Angeles punk band in 1986 as bassist and vocalist and played on five of their studio LPs, including 1990’s Smell The Magic and 1992’s Bricks Are Heavy , two of the grunge era’s essential and most influential albums.
